source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At GetGround, our mission is simple: to make property investment safer, faster, and more accessible for landlords. We believe AI can reshape the landlord journey end to end - not just improving single steps, but reimagining the entire experience of property investing. The result will be a future of buy-to-let that is radically different to how we know it by disrupting a £1.7 trillion UK asset class.



We explore the ideas, predictions, and data trends that will shape this future, and what they mean for landlords and the PropTech industry.



Our four values are the foundation of our culture and shape our behaviours - no BS, pursuit of excellence, feedback obsession and healthy ego. We have built a high performing organisation where intrinsic drive fuels GetGrounders to build a world class investment experience for our users.



In numbers:

 - ➡️ 85+ GetGrounders (ex Google, Meta, Deliveroo, Startups)

 - 🏘 £2bn+ of assets under management

 - 📈 30,000+ users across 70 countries

 - 💲Series A with a strong runway



What's the role?



Building cool things in an environment where are engineers are technically excellent, but also have a passion for product thinking and an ability to operate in a highly commercial environment. You’ll join one of our cross-functional product teams in London, working closely to build user-facing AI-native products in a world where AI is core to how we build software.



How we work



Our engineering culture puts AI as a first-class citizen, not just in the products we ship, but in how we write code, reason about systems, and scale ourselves as engineers.



We look for Full Stack Engineers who are excited to collaborate across the stack, and comfortable working alongside AI tools as their co-pilot in both discovery and delivery.



Some things our Engineers care deeply about:



 - Technical rigour and excellence, with fast feedback loops enabled by collaborative refinement and AI-augmented workflows

 - Clear, thoughtful communication - in code, docs, and discussions

 - Challenging the status quo - exploring new patterns, tools, and ways of working with AI to improve product quality and engineering efficiency

 - Helping shape a product roadmap

 - Thinking commercially about how we build



What you’ll do



 - You’ll work closely cross functionally to understand customer needs, taking ideas from concept all the way through to deployment

 - You’ll enjoy a lot of ownership and freedom, navigating a fast-moving environment where things aren’t always clearly defined

 - Build and maintain user-facing products across the stack - today that means React, Remix, and TypeScript on the frontend, and Golang on the backend - but you’ll work fluidly across both

 - Collaborate closely with product, design, and other engineers to ship high-quality features fast, using AI tools as leverage in your day-to-day development

 - Write clear, maintainable code and thoughtful documentation, for humans and for AI models

 - Help define new engineering practices for an AI-native development environment

 - Mentor other engineers and help us scale our engineering culture



What we’re looking for



 - Experience as a Full Stack Engineer working on user-facing products

 - Product and commercial thinking

 - Comfortable moving between frontend and backend development, even if you have a background leaning more heavily one way

 - A curiosity and excitement about using AI tools to build better software faster

 - Familiarity with React, Remix, and TypeScript is great, but not essential

 - Experience with testing practices - unit, integration, or automated end-to-end tests

 - A track record of mentoring or supporting other engineers



Benefits:

 - 💰 Competitive salary

 - 💰 EMI share options

 - 🏥 Private health

 - 🏥 Private dental

 - 🏖 12 Mental health days off annually (1 per month) PLUS holidays + public holidays

 - 🫶 1 paid Community day off (paid day off to volunteer for a charitable cause)

 - 🏡 Hybrid w

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Customer Support Specialist | Makati, The Philippines

Your work schedule will be Monday to Friday and either: 3pm-12am or 8pm-5am. (PH time)

(Hybrid, 2 days in-office )

We're a VC backed, disruptive technology business with a purpose to make the world fairer and more productive. We are doing this by making assets more transparent, trustworthy and accessible - starting with real estate. Incomes have stagnated and we believe by creating greater access to high value assets like real estate we believe we can be part of solving some of the wealth inequality. Our investment platform is focussed on one of the largest asset classes in the world - property. We are gaining significant momentum with our portfolio of products focused on the UK property investment market.

Our CEO and Founder Moubin https://www.linkedin.com/in/moubin/ previously worked at McKinsey and private equity firm APAX. Check out a podcast with Moubin speaking with our lead investors QED https://open.spotify.com/episode/1O13krBZmgdtU1BadsWxAD about how we're disrupting asset ownership.

Our four values are the foundation of our culture and shape our behaviours - no BS, pursuit of excellence, feedback obsession and healthy ego. We have built a high performing organisation where intrinsic drive fuels GetGrounders to build a world class investment experience for our users.

In numbers:

 - 🧑‍🤝‍🧑 85+ GetGrounders (ex Google, Meta, Deliveroo, Startups)

 - 🏘 £1.5Bn+ of assets being added onto our platform annually

 - 📈 30,000+ users across 70 countries, with ~10% market share

 - 🌎 Global hubs (UK, Dubai, Hong Kong, Singapore, Philippines)

About the role:

The Customer Support team at GetGround is responsible for delivering world-class service to our customers, no matter how big or small their concerns are. Each team member has the power to solve problems, share feedback with the wider team, and make changes to our processes to ensure that we’re delivering great support. Get to know our Director of Operations, Mirella https://medium.com/@GetGround/meet-mirella-director-of-operations-57af71cb9155, and her experience at GetGround here https://medium.com/@GetGround/meet-mirella-director-of-operations-57af71cb9155 https://medium.com/@GetGround/meet-mirella-director-of-operations-57af71cb9155and meet Harry, our Senior Customer Support Specialist here https://medium.com/@GetGround/meet-harry-from-getgrounds-support-team-83f194599923!

What you will do:

 - Build trust in our brand by delivering excellent customer support—primarily over email, but also by phone.

 - Show empathy and care in ensuring customer cases are resolved positively.

 - Manage and prioritise a wide range of customer requests while meeting ambitious SLAs.

 - Collaborate with our onboarding, legal, accounting, compliance, sales, and product teams.

 - Support ad-hoc projects designed to improve the customer experience.

Your experience:

 - Degree or equivilant professional experience

 - Experience in a customer-facing role (internships or part-time jobs are great too).

 - Comfortable communicating with people over phone and email.

 - Clear written and verbal English communication skills.

 - Strong organisational skills and attention to detail.

 - Computer literacy and ability to pick up new tools quickly.

Benefits for GetGround Philippines:

In additional to all Philippine mandatory benefits, we provide:

 - 💰 Competitive Salary

 - 🏥 HMO Benefits

 - 🏥 🦷 Health & Dental Insurance 

 - 🏖 12 Mental Health Days annually + Paid Time Off + Public Holidays

 - 🎳 Team and company-wide events

 - 📈 Learning budget to support your development

 - 🧠 Growth and Development Cycles with performance reviews to enable fast growth

Please note - a member of our Talent team might contact you from a UK mobile number on Viber or directly on your mobile!

What we are building: The first end-to-end real estate investment offering - making the dream of owning real estate more accessible to everyone globally.

Diver

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Growth Marketing Lead to own and scale GetGround's customer acquisition strategy and paid acquisition engine.



Paid media is one of GetGround's most important growth levers, and you'll have end-to-end ownership of strategy, execution, optimisation, and budget allocation across our acquisition channels. You'll be accountable for driving efficient growth through platforms such as Google, Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn, and emerging channels, while continuously identifying opportunities to improve performance and unlock scale.



Beyond channel management, you'll play a key role in shaping acquisition strategy across the wider customer journey. You'll work closely with Product, Design, Engineering, Sales, and Commercial teams to improve conversion, strengthen measurement, develop experimentation programmes, and influence how we invest in growth.



This role is ideal for someone who combines deep hands-on performance marketing expertise with strong commercial thinking and a desire to influence company-wide growth strategy.



Reporting to the Head of Growth Marketing, you'll have significant ownership, budget responsibility, and visibility across the business.




THE OPPORTUNITY

Product-led growth is central to GetGround's ambition to become the default platform for property investors. As our Growth Marketing Lead, you'll own one of the company's most important growth levers while helping shape how we acquire, convert, and retain customers at scale.



You'll work across the full acquisition funnel, from first impression through to conversion, partnering closely with Product, Sales, and Commercial teams to identify growth opportunities and improve performance. You'll be empowered to test new channels, challenge assumptions, influence investment decisions, and drive measurable commercial impact.



This is a high-ownership role with the autonomy to execute, experiment, and shape strategy.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




PAID ACQUISITION LEADERSHIP

 - Own the strategy, execution, optimisation, and scaling of paid acquisition across channels including Google, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, and emerging platforms

 - Manage and allocate budgets to maximise commercial outcomes while balancing efficiency and growth

 - Develop channel strategies aligned to company objectives and growth targets

 - Maintain best-in-class account structures, campaign frameworks, and operational processes

 - Identify and evaluate new acquisition channels and opportunities for investment

 - Act as the internal subject matter expert on paid acquisition and platform best practices




GROWTH STRATEGY & PLANNING

 - Own acquisition strategy across the customer journey, identifying the highest-leverage opportunities for growth

 - Develop growth plans that balance efficiency, scale, experimentation, and commercial objectives

 - Build business cases for new initiatives, channels, and growth investments

 - Partner with leadership on forecasting, budget planning, and target setting

 - Translate business objectives into measurable acquisition strategies and execution plans

 - Monitor market trends, competitor activity, and platform developments to inform future growth opportunities




PERFORMANCE & EXPERIMENTATION

 - Continuously analyse campaign and funnel performance to identify opportunities for improvement

 - Design and execute structured experiments across targeting, creative, messaging, bidding strategies, landing pages, and conversion journeys

 - Partner with Product teams to improve activation, conversion, and customer experience across key journeys

 - Collaborate with Design to develop and iterate high-performing creative concepts

 - Drive a culture of testing, learning, and evidence-based decision making




MEASUREMENT & INSIGHTS

 - Maintain a deep understanding of attribution, incrementality, and modern measurement approaches

 - Work closely with Data and Engineering teams to improve tracking, reporting, and measurement infrastruct

source_jobs.title: Compliance and Risk Lead
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Compliance and Risk Lead to be the hands-on engine of GetGround's compliance function, owning the day-to-day delivery of our regulatory obligations and helping build the frameworks that keep us operating with integrity as we scale.




THE OPPORTUNITY

GetGround operates at the intersection of fintech, proptech and wealthtech, a business with a complex and growing regulatory footprint. With £2Bn+ in assets on platform and 30,000+ users across 70 countries, our compliance obligations are significant and expanding.

This is a role with real ownership and some people management responsibility. You'll be doing the work and leading a small team to help deliver it. You'll be the person who makes compliance and risk happen at GetGround.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING




DAY-TO-DAY COMPLIANCE

 - Own the delivery of GetGround's compliance monitoring programme, including reviews, testing, and reporting

 - Develop and manage a high-performing compliance and risk team as GetGround scales

 - Maintain and update compliance policies, procedures, and controls as regulations evolve

 - Support the management of regulatory relationships, including FCA interactions and reporting

 - Track regulatory developments and translate them into clear, actionable guidance for the business

 - Assist with the preparation of board and senior leadership compliance reporting




RISK

 - Support the identification, assessment, and monitoring of operational and regulatory risks across the business

 - Maintain the risk register and assist with preparing risk reporting for senior leadership

 - Work with teams across the business to ensure risk considerations are embedded into day-to-day decisions

 - Help develop and maintain risk policies and frameworks as the business grows




FINANCIAL CRIME AND AML

 - Support the MLRO with day-to-day AML and financial crime operations, including transaction monitoring and sanctions screening

 - Conduct and review KYC processes, escalating complex cases as appropriate

 - Assist with SAR filing processes and maintain accurate financial crime records

 - Help ensure GetGround's financial crime controls remain fit for purpose as the business grows
   
   


BUSINESS PARTNERSHIP

 - Work with Product and Engineering teams to review new features and products for compliance implications

 - Support the review of customer-facing content, marketing materials, and regulated communications

 - Deliver compliance training and awareness across the business

 - Be the first point of contact for compliance queries from across the business




ABOUT YOU

You're a hands-on compliance professional who knows how to get things done in a fast-moving, regulated environment. You bring:

 - Solid experience in a compliance role within an FCA-regulated firm, ideally in fintech, payments, mortgages, or property finance

 - Good working knowledge of FCA regulations, including Consumer Duty, AML, and KYC requirements

 - Experience running or supporting a compliance monitoring programme

 - Familiarity with financial crime controls, including transaction monitoring and sanctions screening

 - A practical, solutions-oriented approach; you find ways to make compliance work for the business

 - Strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities at once

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills



Highly desirable:

 - Experience with property, mortgage, or investment products

 - Exposure to MLRO support or deputy MLRO responsibilities

 - In-house experience at a fast-growing fintech



We know that great candidates don't always match every point on a job description. If this role excites you and you meet most of the criteria, we'd encourage you to apply anyway.



What we are building: The first end-to-end real estate investment offering - making the dream of owning real estate more accessible to everyone globally.

Diversity & inclusion at GetGround: We encourage applications from all sections of so

source_jobs.title: Senior Legal Counsel
source_jobs.description:
Senior Legal Counsel


We're looking for a Senior Legal Counsel to be a core part of GetGround's legal function, providing sharp, commercially minded legal advice across the business as we scale.

You'll be doing the legal work, advising on commercial contracts, product questions, regulatory matters, and everything in between. You'll be a trusted partner to teams across the business and a key part of how we manage legal risk day-to-day.



THE OPPORTUNITY

GetGround operates at the intersection of fintech, proptech and wealthtech. We have £2Bn+ in assets on platform, 30,000+ users across 70 countries, and a portfolio of regulated products spanning mortgages, property management, and investment tools.

As we continue to grow, our legal needs are growing with us. You'll play a central role in keeping the business legally sound, moving quickly, and operating with integrity. For a lawyer who wants to work on genuinely varied, interesting matters in a fast-paced environment, this is a strong opportunity.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING


Commercial and Contracts

 - Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts, including supplier agreements, partnership contracts, and customer terms

 - Advise business teams on contractual risk and help them make informed decisions quickly

 - Maintain and improve GetGround's contract templates, playbooks, and standard terms

 - Support the management of external counsel on matters requiring specialist input
   
   

Product and Regulatory

 - Provide legal input on new product features, customer journeys, and platform changes

 - Advise on FCA regulatory requirements as they apply to GetGround's products and services

 - Support compliance reviews of customer-facing content and regulated communications

 - Help track relevant regulatory developments and flag implications for the business
   
   

Corporate and General Legal

 - Support on corporate matters including fundraising, corporate governance, and company secretarial work

 - Advise on data protection and privacy matters, including GDPR compliance

 - Provide general legal support across the business on employment, IP, and operational matters

 - Help build scalable legal processes, templates, and training materials
   
   

ABOUT YOU


You're a UK qualified solicitor with around 4+ years PQE, ideally with a mix of private practice and some in-house exposure. You bring:

 - Strong commercial legal skills, with experience drafting and negotiating contracts in a fast-paced environment

 - A good understanding of FCA regulation and how it applies to fintech or financial services products

 - Intellectual curiosity and the ability to pick up new areas of law quickly

 - A commercial mindset; you give practical advice, not just legal analysis

 - The confidence to work autonomously and manage your own workload without close supervision

 - Clear, concise communication skills, written and verbal

 - A collaborative approach and genuine interest in the business, not just the legal matters

Highly desirable:

 - Experience with property, mortgage, or investment products

 - In-house experience at a fast-growing fintech

 - Exposure to data protection, GDPR, or privacy work

We know that great candidates don't always match every point on a job description. If this role excites you and you meet most of the criteria, we'd encourage you to apply anyway.


WHAT WE'RE BUILDING

The first end-to-end real estate investment offering, making the dream of owning property accessible to everyone, globally. With £2Bn+ assets on our platform and 30,000+ users across 70 countries, we're building the future of asset ownership and in doing so, helping to address wealth inequality.


DIVERSITY & INCLUSION AT GETGROUND

We encourage applications from all sections of society and believe in the criticality of an inclusive culture. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability,

source_jobs.title: Lead Analytics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
London, Waterloo (Hybrid, 4 days in-office, Wednesday is our set work-from-home day, though you can come in on Wednesday too if you wish)



We're disrupting one of the world's largest asset classes, property. With £4Bn+ assets on our platform and 30,000+ users across 70 countries, we're building the future of asset ownership and, in doing so, addressing wealth inequality.

Our product simplifies property investing from start to finish, making real estate investment accessible to everyone.




THE OPPORTUNITY

Our ambition is to hold the most complete context of any property investor anywhere (every decision, document, transaction, and signal) so that whatever an investor needs to be successful can happen inside GetGround. Good decisions need complete context. That's the data problem we're solving.

We have a data architecture that already collects, joins, curates, and surfaces the data we generate. The next phase is harder. We need data that's clear, unambiguous, trusted, and usable across the business (not just by the technically confident). We need to simplify how we work with it. And because we ship quickly, the data layer has to keep pace with logic and schema changes rather than fall behind them.

We're looking for someone curious and outcome-driven to lead this work. You'll inherit the function from our outgoing Lead Analytics Engineer, so you'll need to land, ramp quickly, and start making decisions. You'll be the data function for a while, with the remit to define how it grows. You'll report to our Product Director and partner closely with Product, Engineering, Growth, RevOps, and Finance.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Building trust in our data. Right now, getting the right answer requires knowing the business AND the data. Your job is to close that gap so anyone (including AI-assisted users) can get reliable answers.

 - Owning the analytics platform end-to-end (from raw sources through to the metrics that power business-facing reporting).

 - Diagnosing problems wherever they live. Symptoms in one layer don't always have causes in the same layer. You'll need to be able to follow a data quality issue back to its source (and decide where to fix it).

 - Simplifying, not stacking. We’re fine with fewer tools and clearer data, not more layers on top.

 - Modelling our core entities properly. Users, ownership structures, properties, transactions (the data model that underpins everything we report on, with the nuance our business actually needs).

 - Governing the metrics layer. From Finance, to Product, Growth, and Compliance.

 - Writing clear, maintainable code and thoughtful documentation (for humans and for AI models). Setting the engineering bar for our data culture as the team grows beneath you.

 - Mentoring and hiring. Within ~12 months we'd expect you to be hiring and developing an Analyst or second Analytics Engineer beneath you, and lifting the data literacy of product and engineering teams across the business.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



 - Systems thinking and first-principles reasoning. You map before you act. You're comfortable saying "this is the wrong question" and reframing. You spot root causes other people miss because they're looking at the symptom.

 - A bias for simplification. You've removed more code and tools than you've added. You can tell the difference between essential and accidental complexity, and you push hard on the second.

 - Engineering across the stack, not just the warehouse. You can read application code and reason about it. You can navigate a Postgres or MySQL schema and tell what's well-designed from what's accreted over time. You're comfortable opening a pull request against a production codebase to fix a data problem at source, not just patching it downstream.

 - Strong engineering fundamentals. You write clean, tested, maintainable code; you understand the systems your data flows through; you can read a schema and reason about why it was designed the way it was.

 - Strong SQ

source_jobs.title: Junior Accountant
source_jobs.description:
(Please note this role is with TPSSI, GetGround's partner. We work Monday to Friday, 11AM to 8PM local time in Makati City to support our partner GetGround in the UK)

TPSSI - who we are
Terranova Philippines Support Services Inc. (“TPSSI”) is a team of currently 30 colleagues (and growing!) that was formed to support our UK-based partner, GetGround. TPSSI operations are led by Mike https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-angelo-vergara-099301148/ (Makati) and Dan https://www.linkedin.com/in/daniele-mattia/ (UK). We are expanding the team of accountants significantly in the next 3 months.

👉 Here's some content from our Makati team (video, blog posts, podcasts): https://linktr.ee/getground_philippines

GetGround is a values-driven business with the purpose of making the world fairer and more productive by making investing in high value assets like real estate more accessible. GetGround has 30,000+ real estate customers globally with £1.5Bn+ real estate value in the platform.

Benefits - in additional to all Philippine mandatory benefits, we provide:

 - 💰 Competitive Salary

 - 🏥 HMO Benefits

 - 🏥 🦷 Health & Dental Insurance 

 - 🏖 12 Mental Health Days annually

 - 🏖 10 days SIL / Paid Time Off + Philippines Public Holidays

 - 📚 2 weeks of fully paid study leave for your growth and development

 - 🏡 1 day working from home per week (after passing your probation)

 - 🎳 Team and company-wide events

 - 📈 Learning budget to support your development

 - 🧠 Growth and Development Cycles with performance reviews to enable fast growth

What we’re looking for:

 - Degree in an accounting-related university course

 - <1 year of accounting-related work experience (fresh graduates welcome!)

 - Willing to work out of our Makati office, 4 days per week

What you will do:

 - Request financial information and documents from clients

 - Perform data entry and year-end closing transactions

 - Draft working papers

 - Submit reports to client and appropriate government authorities

 - Deliver world-class service with the support our your team

Please note - you might be called from a UK number on Viber! This will be a member of our Talent team

What we are building: The first end-to-end real estate investment offering - making the dream of owning real estate more accessible to everyone globally.

Diversity & inclusion at GetGround: We encourage applications from all sections of society and we believe in the criticality of an inclusive culture. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other basis as protected by law.

 - 42% of our employees identify as female or non-specified, 58% as male

 - 22 nationalities represented across offices in 5 countries

 - Our work on Design Accessibility https://medium.com/@GetGround/design-that-works-for-everyone-accessibility-at-getground-a7cf7ca867f0

 - Inclusion is at the heart of our culture - we celebrate and reflect on key D&I and cultural events such as: Black History Month, International Women's Day and Pride

For more information on how we store your candidate data, please see our recruitment privacy policy https://25516403.fs1.hubspotusercontent-eu1.net/hubfs/25516403/GetGroundRecruitmentPrivacyPolicy.pdf.

source_jobs.title: Legal and Compliance Director / Senior Director
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Legal and Compliance Director / Senior Director to own and build our legal, regulatory and compliance function as GetGround scales its regulated financial services and property investment platform. With ambitious plans to grow into new markets, this is a role for a highly commercial lawyer who has operated at a senior level, built or led a legal and compliance function, and is ready to help further shape the whole thing.



You'll shape how a fast-growing business that operates at the intersection of fintech, proptech and wealthtech, manages risk, meets its regulatory obligations, and earns the trust of customers, regulators, and partners, including as we look beyond our current markets.



This role reports directly to the COO.




THE OPPORTUNITY

GetGround has grown significantly since we last hired in this function. We now have £2Bn+ in assets on platform, 30,000+ users across 70 countries, and a portfolio of regulated products spanning mortgages, property management, and investment tools. Our ambition is to keep growing, including into new geographies, and our legal and regulatory function needs to be ready for that.



You'll be shaping the function, not just running it. That means continuing to set the strategy, build frameworks that can scale into new markets, support the team, and being the person the business turns to when the stakes are high.



You’ll champion the use of AI and legal technology tools across the function, from contract review and regulatory research to compliance monitoring to make us a faster, more scalable team




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING




LEGAL LEADERSHIP

 - Own all legal matters across corporate, commercial, IP, employment, data, product, and fundraising

 - Drive and support geographic expansion, including navigating legal and regulatory requirements in new markets

 - Lead and negotiate high-value commercial partnerships, customer and supplier contracts

 - Advise the CEO and leadership team on legal risk as we launch new products and enter new markets

 - Manage external counsel relationships and budgets with rigour and commercial discipline

 - Build scalable legal infrastructure: templates, processes, playbooks, and training
   
   


REGULATORY COMPLIANCE

 - Own GetGround's compliance with FCA regulations across all products and markets, including Consumer Duty, COBS, and MCOB

 - Act as (or appoint and oversee) the Compliance Officer and MLRO, with full accountability for AML, KYC, and financial crime controls

 - Lead proactive engagement with the FCA and other regulatory bodies, and build the relationships and frameworks that will underpin future market entry

 - Ensure all regulated communications, marketing, and customer-facing content meet compliance standards

 - Keep the business ahead of regulatory change, not scrambling to catch up
   
   


RISK MANAGEMENT

 - Design and own the enterprise risk management framework, including risk appetite, policies, governance, and reporting. Leading risk identification, assessment, and mitigation across all business areas
   
   


BUILDING THE TEAM AND CULTURE

 - Develop and manage a high-performing legal and compliance team as GetGround scales

 - Foster a compliance culture that enables the business rather than blocking it: pragmatic, proportionate, and trusted

 - Be a visible partner across every function: Finance, Product, Engineering, Operations, and Marketing
   
   


ABOUT YOU

You are a qualified solicitor with significant post-qualification experience and crucially, you have moved well beyond pure legal delivery. You are deeply commercial, a leader, and someone who can run the whole legal, compliance, and risk function independently. You bring:

 - Senior legal leadership experience: 6 + Years of PQE experience and experience heading up the legal function

 - Commercial instincts: you think like a business person first and a lawyer second, and you are trusted by leadership to make calls that balance risk with

notes: GetGround is a fintech and proptech company with a strong focus on product development and innovation.
